---
title: "Projects"
---

## Project library

Projects are larger programs that combine several ideas from the lessons.

::: {.callout-tip}
## How to use projects

Try to build the first version yourself. Then improve it by adding your own features.
:::

## Mini projects in this course

::: {.lesson-grid}

::: {.lesson-card}
### Mini Project 1: Quiz Game

Build a three-question quiz that checks answers and keeps score.

Skills used:

- variables
- `input()`
- `int()`
- `if`, `else`, and `elif`

[Open Mini Project 1](mini_project_1_quiz_game.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Mini Project 2: Guess the Number

Build a guessing game that uses a loop and counts guesses.

Skills used:

- variables
- `input()`
- `int()`
- `while`
- `if`, `elif`, and `else`

[Open Mini Project 2](mini_project_2_guess_the_number.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Mini Project 3: Dice Game

Build a dice battle game using randomness, functions and loops.

Skills used:

- `random.randint()`
- functions
- loops
- scorekeeping
- `if`, `elif`, and `else`

[Open Mini Project 3](mini_project_3_dice_game.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Mini Project 4: Flashcard Trainer

Build a study tool that asks questions, checks answers and saves scores.

Skills used:

- dictionaries
- files
- functions
- loops
- randomness
- debugging

[Open Mini Project 4](mini_project_4_flashcard_trainer.qmd)
:::

:::

## Future project ideas

### Shopping Receipt

Ask for item prices and quantities. Print a neat receipt.

### Mini Adventure Game

The player chooses what to do next and the story changes.

### Calculator Toolkit

Use functions to create a reusable calculator.

### High Score Table

Save scores to a file and load them again later.

### Word Guessing Game

Use strings, lists, loops and randomness to build a word game.

## Project template

Use this structure for new projects:

```markdown
---
title: "Project name"
---

## Goal

What should the program do?

## Skills you will use

- Skill 1
- Skill 2

## What the program should do

Describe the target behaviour.

## Step 1

Build the smallest working version.

## Step 2

Add one feature.

## Challenge extensions

Add optional improvements.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Click to reveal hints

Put hints here.

:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Click to reveal one possible solution

Put a full solution here.

:::

## What you practised

Summarise what the learner practised.
```
